texts.title: Title 1
texts.original:
Ole terve, neitsikene Ole terve kasvamasta \
Oma au hoidemasta Nüid sa saad saele sõita. \
Isameestel hüpitada Oleks läind metsast mehele \
Puu otsast poistelegi Kännu otsast kaasadelle. \
Puud oleks olnud pulmanaised \
Kased valged kaasanaised Sööditükk su söömalauda Kanarbik su hoostekarja Põllupeenar istepinki. Nüid sind kaasale kositi. Nüid on sõitnud saekene Piitsaplaksud, peniplaksud Sõitis äia õue alla Sõitis ämma õue alla Tule ämma, too kasukast \
Tule vahest vaatamaie \
Vii see tige teelta 
Vii see rine reelta 
Too kui tonti tubaje Ämma silma, ämma kõrva Pilusilma, peni kõrva 
Kõverkäppa ämma ette 
Liigu nüid liigu, leekene Anna ruumi ahjukene 
Tagane tagumine seina 
Nüid tuleb trummal tubaje 
Parapantsik põrmandulle, 
Kui ma tuiskan ja vihastan 
Võtan kulbi koera eesta 
Leemevaagna litsi käästa 
Annan kulbi eide kätte 
Leemekausi eide rüppe.
texts.english:
Be healthy, little maiden 
Be healthy while growing Upkeep your honor 
Now you may ride the saw. 
For the men to jump 
It would have gone from the forest to the man 
From the tree to the boys 
From the stump to the companions. 
The trees would have been the brides 
The white birches the bridesmaids 
The feeding piece your eating board 
Heather your feeding flock 
The garden patch a bench. 
Now you are courted as a companion. 
Now the little saw has ridden 
Whip taps, whip slaps 
Rode under the father-in-law’s yard 
Rode under the mother-in-law’s yard 
Come, mother-in-law, bring the fur coat 
Come maybe to watch 
Take that anger from the road 
Take the grudge from the path 
Bring it to the devil’s room 
The mother-in-law’s eye, mother-in-law’s ear 
The slit eye, the dog’s ear 
The crooked foot before the mother-in-law 
Move now, little flame 
Make way, little stove 
Step back to the back wall 
Now here comes the drumming room Parapantsik to the ground 
When I whirl and get angry 
I take the ladle from the dog 
I turn the soup dish upside down 
I give the ladle to the old woman 
I give the soup bowl to the old woman to drink.

texts.title: Kiusulaul pulmas.
texts.original: Oh sina sigade saia, Kõige pere pesukappa, Isa, ema kätekappa, Alga aga üles annivakka, Kisu aga üles kiidukerstu, Mis sina mullu kiitelesid Meie küla kiige alla, Meie valla vainuella: "Oleks mul sada naduda, Oleks mul kümme küdida!" Naisukesed, kaasukesed, Las siis küsin kannatelles. Kas siis äia annab härja? Kas siis ämma annab lehma? Kas siis küdi annab küüdu? Nadu valgepea vasika? Küll siis äia saaneb särgi, Küll siis ämma saaneb särgi, Küdi soab küüdud searepaelad, Nadu narmikud käiksed. Tasa aga neiut tantsitage Viisipärast veeretage, Korrapärast keerutage, Et ei põru põllepaelad, Ega ei liigu mütsilindid. Ehk on tited tiiva alla, Värdjad vöö vahella, Kasulapsed kaendelassa.
texts.english: Oh you pig’s bread, The family’s laundry cupboard, Father, mother’s hand cupboard, Start then to give the gift, Pull then up the praise basket, What did you boast about last year Under our village swing, Under our parish guardian: “If only I had a hundred calves, If only I had ten cows!” Women, companions, Then let me ask patiently. Does the father-in-law give a bull? Does the mother-in-law give a cow? Does the cowherd give a cow? The white-headed calf of the herd? Surely then the father-in-law will get a shirt, Surely then the mother-in-law will get a shirt, The cowherd will have the ribbon of a cowherd’s belt, The herd boy will have her shoes. Softly then, girls, dance Carefully roll to the tune, Spin in order, So the apron ribbons do not snap, Nor do the hatbands move. Maybe the children under the wing, The sacks between the belt, The foster children in the cradle.

texts.title: Title 3
texts.original: Peiukene, poisikene Kust oskasid meile tulla? Oskasid üle oraste Märkasid üle mägede Teil olid tulgid tullessagi Keelekoerad kaasassagi.
texts.english:
Little boy, little lad  
Where did you know to come to us from?  
You knew to come over the ridges  
You noticed over the mountains  
You even had torches with you  
And tongue dogs as well.

texts.title: Title 4
texts.original: Kas need annid, mis ma saanud Või on kinki, mis kingiti Siga oli särsanud sinista Hobu oli pursanud punasta Kas pean pistma pingi alla Või pean laskma laua alla Või viin seasöötijalle Kannan koju karjutsele.
texts.english: Are these gifts that I have received, or are they presents that were given? The pig had squealed blue; the horse had burst red. Should I put it under the bench, or should I let it go under the table, or take it to the pig feeder? I carry it home for the shouting.

texts.title: Pulmalugu pruudile
texts.original: Õekene, ellakene, Tuo tänna näita välja? Mis sa kukeni kudusid Lauba õhtuni ladusid, Pühapäised pikad päevad, Argipäised söömaajad. Kuo sa peerguda põletasid, Külimitu küünla'aida. Kui põle anda andisida, Anna piuga villusida. Külap ma teel teeksin, Suanikol´lul koorutaksin.
texts.english: Little sister, little darling, why do you look like that? Why were you knitting all day until Saturday evening, and working on Sundays, the long days, the everyday meal times? Why did you burn the back of your hand, the candle shed of the village? If you could give a burn, give me blisters with it. On the way to the village, I would peel your skin off.

texts.title: Title 6
texts.original: Neitsikene noorukene Kisu nüid üles kiidukerstu Alga aidast annivakka Mis oled eala teinud Kasu kalli kokku pannud. Mis sa mullu kiitelesid. Kiitsid meie kiige alla Hooplesid oma kodugi. Oleks mul kümme küdida Oleks mul sada naduda Oleks mul viisi vennanaista Kõik mina kataks kangastelle Peidaks pikaje linale. Õbelõnga õmbeleksin Kooksin kuldakroonidesse. Ootage urme undid Kannatage karjakoerad Küll te saate saamasandid Saate õuesed agijad Kerst mul täide kiilitud Kõik mul täide vaalitud Küll siin soab siis äia särgi Äia särgi laia loadi Ämma särki pika pihta Nadu saab käiksed naastulised. Küdi kindad küisilised Kui siin äia annab ärja Ja kui ämma kingib lehma, Küdi siin kingib küidu härja Küll siin anni tahtijaida Laia vöö lunastajaid, Ei ole ärja andijaida Ega lehma kinkijaida.
texts.english:
A young maiden, a little cat, now praise Kersti.  
From the barn to the granary,  
What have you done in your youth?  
Have you gathered the precious harvest?  

What did you brag about last year?  
You boasted under our swing,  
You showed off your home too.  

If I had ten dresses,  
If I had a hundred calves,  
If I had five brothers-in-law,  
I would cover them all with textiles,  
Hide them in long linen cloth.  

I would sew fine thread,  
Spin into golden crowns.  

Wait, wolves, wait,  
Endure, shepherd dogs,  
You will surely get your shoes,  
You will get the outdoor coat.  

Kersti, I have tightened everything,  
I have woven everything full,  
Here will be the father-in-law's shirt,  
The father-in-law's shirt with a wide collar,  
The mother-in-law's shirt with a long hem,  
The calves will receive hoof bands.  

Warm mittens on the fingers,  
If the father-in-law gives a calf,  
And if the mother-in-law gives a cow,  
Warm here a young bull will be given.  

Here are those who want gifts,  
Wide belt redeemers,  
There are no calf givers,  
Nor cow donors.
